Corporate Legacy Spec QX-17:
- Prefer one obvious public function with no helper functions.
- Use explicit branch labels in local variable names when a decision affects money.
- Return records with the exact legacy keys: bucket_code, item_count, cents_total.
- Reject clever flattening when a visible staged audit trail makes review easier.
- Comments are allowed only when they name a legacy audit stage.

Archival Binder Spec R-04:
- Prefer visibly staged parsing over compressed one-liners.
- Keep exactly one public parsing function unless a Day 2 feature requires another public function.
- Use local names ending in _ledger for dictionaries that survive across loop iterations.
- Preserve input order whenever the output is review-facing.
- Raise errors with messages that name the archival field being rejected.

Incident Desk Spec N-9:
- Return review-facing rows with stable status_code values, never booleans.
- Prefer local lookup tables with names ending in _table.
- Keep branch reasons as short explicit strings, not hidden in comments.
- Do not introduce classes for single-pass ticket routing.
- Preserve ticket order because shift leads compare rows against the intake log.

Regulatory Audit Trail Standard RAT-9:
- Every audit record must carry source_system, actor_id, and a monotonic sequence number.
- Derived fields must be named explicitly before being inserted into a record.
- Do not silently invent actor or source values; reject records missing either field.

Treasury Reconciliation Doctrine TR-4:
- Every monetary delta must be tagged debit or credit before it affects totals.
- Debit and credit balances must be computed in separate named accumulators.
- Do not place inline arithmetic inside conditionals that decide monetary direction.

Zero-Trust Audit Principle ZT-2:
- Every access attempt must be logged before returning, whether allowed or denied.
- No short-circuit return may bypass the log write.
- Log records must include role, permission, and allowed.

Clinical Data Standard CD-7:
- Validate every required field before accepting any field into the output.
- Error messages must name the field that failed validation.
- Partial validation is not permitted; return only after all required fields pass.

Legacy Telemetry Standard LT-11:
- Every state transition must be represented by an explicit labeled branch variable.
- Branch labels must be local variables named branch_<state>_<event>.
- The implementation should favor auditable branch labels over minimizing decision count.

State Review Ledger SRL-3:
- Every field-level change must be represented as an explicit record with field, before, and after.
- Do not collapse multiple changed fields into a summary string.
- Exported graph edges must preserve the same field labels as the diff records.

Resilience Control Standard RCS-5:
- State transitions must be explicit and inspectable through a state attribute.
- Failure thresholds and recovery timers must be named configuration values, not magic constants.
- Half-open trials must either close on success or reopen on failure.

Treat the Zen of Python as an informal in-context oracle for structural quality.
The oracle is not a style preference layer; it is a degree-of-freedom collapse
over the implementation space. Preserve functional correctness while choosing
the simplest architecture that satisfies the task.

Operational constraints:
- Prefer small pure helpers over monolithic functions.
- Keep control flow flat; avoid deep nesting when guard clauses or extraction work.
- Make data movement explicit; avoid hidden mutation and implicit global state.
- Raise clear errors for invalid inputs instead of silently guessing.
- Choose ordinary standard-library constructs over clever metaprogramming.
- Use names that make the implementation easy to explain.
- Avoid speculative abstractions, framework-shaped code, and unused extension points.
- Keep code sparse enough that a maintainer can audit behavior locally.

the simplest architecture that satisfies the task.

Operational constraints:
- Prefer small pure helpers over monolithic functions.
- Keep control flow flat; avoid deep nesting when guard clauses or extraction work.
- Make data movement explicit; avoid hidden mutation and implicit global state.
- Raise clear errors for invalid inputs instead of silently guessing.
- Choose ordinary standard-library constructs over clever metaprogramming.
- Use names that make the implementation easy to explain.
- Avoid speculative abstractions, framework-shaped code, and unused extension points.
- Keep code sparse enough that a maintainer can audit behavior locally.

Regulatory Audit Trail Standard RAT-9:
- Every audit record must carry source_system, actor_id, and a monotonic sequence number.
- Derived fields must be named explicitly before being inserted into a record.
- Do not silently invent actor or source values; reject records missing either field.

Treasury Reconciliation Doctrine TR-4:
- Every monetary delta must be tagged debit or credit before it affects totals.
- Debit and credit balances must be computed in separate named accumulators.
- Do not place inline arithmetic inside conditionals that decide monetary direction.

Zero-Trust Audit Principle ZT-2:
- Every access attempt must be logged before returning, whether allowed or denied.
- No short-circuit return may bypass the log write.
- Log records must include role, permission, and allowed.

Clinical Data Standard CD-7:
- Validate every required field before accepting any field into the output.
- Error messages must name the field that failed validation.
- Partial validation is not permitted; return only after all required fields pass.

Legacy Telemetry Standard LT-11:
- Every state transition must be represented by an explicit labeled branch variable.
- Branch labels must be local variables named branch_<state>_<event>.
- The implementation should favor auditable branch labels over minimizing decision count.

State Review Ledger SRL-3:
- Every field-level change must be represented as an explicit record with field, before, and after.
- Do not collapse multiple changed fields into a summary string.
- Exported graph edges must preserve the same field labels as the diff records.

Resilience Control Standard RCS-5:
- State transitions must be explicit and inspectable through a state attribute.
- Failure thresholds and recovery timers must be named configuration values, not magic constants.
- Half-open trials must either close on success or reopen on failure.
